Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Note Streamlit Cloud cache limitation in README

The file-based enrichment cache only persists locally. On Streamlit
Cloud the filesystem is ephemeral and resets on redeploy or sleep,
so the cache is session-scoped there rather than run-to-run persistent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stable component identity for cross-process WAL/snapshot (#47)

* Add stable component identity design doc

Design for cross-process WAL/snapshot compatibility: CodecRegistry
gains name-based lookup, WAL gains schema preamble, receivers remap
sender-local ComponentIds via stable string names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add stable component identity implementation plan

8-task TDD plan: CodecRegistry by_name, WAL schema preamble,
build_remap, snapshot name resolution, caller updates,
backwards compatibility, cross-process end-to-end test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): CodecRegistry::register_as + by_name lookup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): WalEntry, WalSchema, WalComponentDef record types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): build_remap for cross-process ComponentId resolution

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): WAL schema preamble + ID remapping on replay

Wal::create now writes a WalEntry::Schema preamble recording all
registered components with their stable names and layouts. Wal::open
accepts &CodecRegistry for API symmetry. append() wraps records in
WalEntry::Mutations. replay_from() parses the schema preamble, builds
a ComponentId remap via CodecRegistry::build_remap(), and applies it
to every mutation during replay. The persist example now uses
register_as() with explicit stable names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(persist): snapshot save/load with stable name resolution + ID remapping

- build_snapshot_data uses codecs.stable_name(id) for schema names,
  falling back to world.component_name(id) for non-codec components
- restore_world and restore_world_zero_copy now build a remap table
  from the snapshot schema via codecs.build_remap(), enabling
  cross-process snapshot loading with different registration orders
- Components are registered in schema order to preserve ID slots;
  remap is used only for codec lookups (deserialize/layout), while
  sender IDs are used for record_spawn in the fresh world
- Add cross-process round-trip tests for both standard and zero-copy
  load paths with reversed registration order

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(persist): backwards compatibility + cross-process WAL replay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix review findings: remap soundness, type unification, test coverage

Critical:
- remap_id errors on unmapped ID when remap exists (was silent fallback)
- Rename misleading legacy WAL test — new format is intentionally incompatible

Important:
- Unify WalComponentDef into ComponentSchema (identical types)
- Remove duplicate name()/stable_name() — keep stable_name() only
- Use unique __unnamed_{id} placeholders instead of "unknown"
- Add cross-process WAL test for Insert/Remove remap paths
- Add cross-process snapshot test with sparse components

Suggestions:
- Sort registered_ids() for deterministic schema preamble order
- Add #[cfg(test)] mod tests to record.rs
- Test idempotent re-registration and same-type-different-name panic
- Test alignment-only mismatch in build_remap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add safety comment explaining intentional WAL format break

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix IntegrityError when creating monitors in global view v3.13.78

require_organization_context had a fall-through bug: when a POST arrived
without _selected_organization_id, it added an error message but then
continued to call the view anyway. The view ran with org=None, passed
it to monitor.organization, and save() raised IntegrityError on the
NOT NULL organization_id column.

Fix: after any POST path that doesn't resolve a valid org, redirect back
to the same URL as a GET. The view is never called with org=None on POST.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: MM v4 (5 fixes) + P&L position size fallback fix

Market Maker v4 — 5 strategy fixes (still observe_only=True):
- Fix 1: Both-side quoting by default, directional skew only at >0.70 conf
  Was 100% one-side (wrong prediction = 100% adverse), now true market making
- Fix 2: Inventory decay 30s→10s, hard kill at 15s, repost interval 10s→2.5s
  BTC at $68K, 30s hold is an eternity — cut exposure window 3x
- Fix 3: Adverse flow cancel — 3 consecutive Binance trades against in 500ms
  triggers immediate exit instead of waiting 5-30s to discover bad fill
- Fix 4: Min spread gate 2bps→4bps (need ≥4bps to cover 7bps RT fees)
- Fix 5: Volume imbalance filter — don't quote into 3:1 momentum steamrolls

P&L position size fixes:
- outcome_tracker fallback: 1.0 BTC → $1000/entry (~0.015 BTC)
  Trades with NULL would_position_size were getting 70x overstated P&L
- Solo-engine would_position_size: 0 → 1000.0 (shadow floor)
  Zero triggered the 1.0 BTC fallback, making solo P&L ~70x wrong

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: strip markdown fences from LLM JSON responses

Google Gemini wraps JSON output in ```json ... ``` fences even when
response_mime_type is set to application/json. The parser now strips
these fences before json.loads(), enabling Google as a working provider.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Temper: LoadAnvilConfig swallows non-ENOENT errors and prints to stderr (Forge-kna) (#108)

* fix: add tests for Temper LoadAnvilConfig error handling (Forge-kna)

LoadAnvilConfig already correctly returns errors for non-ENOENT cases
(permission errors, corrupt YAML), but had no test coverage. Add tests
to verify:
- Returns (nil, nil) when file doesn't exist
- Parses valid YAML correctly
- Returns error for corrupt YAML (not silently swallowed)
- Returns error for non-ENOENT read failures

Bead: Forge-kna

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add tests for Temper LoadAnvilConfig error handling (Forge-kna)

Fix TestLoadAnvilConfig_ReturnsErrorForUnreadableFile to work on Windows
by making temper.yaml a directory (produces non-ENOENT error on all
platforms) instead of making .forge a file (Windows treats path-not-found
as ENOENT via os.IsNotExist).

Bead: Forge-kna

fix: Migrate Pedidos UI from client Firestore SDK to API routes

All dashboard components now use fetch('/api/...') instead of direct
Firestore client SDK calls. This fixes PERMISSION_DENIED errors for
non-admin users (atendente/producao) whose Firestore security rules
block direct client-side access.

Migrated components:
- PedidoList: fetchPedidos → GET /api/pedidos
- PedidoContext: fetchPedidoById → GET /api/pedidos/[id]
- PedidoForm: fetchStoreSettings → GET /api/store-settings
- Edit page: updatePedido → PUT /api/pedidos/[id]
- FreightSettings: read/write → /api/store-settings
- StoreHoursEditor: read/write → /api/store-hours
- StoreAddressList: full CRUD → /api/store-addresses

commit.message:
Fix galaxy graph crash + resilient stranded recovery

Galaxy:
- findNearest() null-checks neighbor nodes before accessing .system
  (fixes crash when graph has partial data — neighbor in list but not loaded)

Stranded recovery:
- Wrap isStranded() calls in try-catch with fallback (fuel <= 2)
- Prevents Vex Castellan II crash loop at dheneb system

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove marketplace.json to prevent recursive cache nesting (#1)

The repo is a single plugin, not a marketplace (registry of plugins).
Having marketplace.json caused Claude Code to treat it as both,
creating duplicate entries in marketplaces/ and cache/ that recurse
on reload. Removing marketplace.json fixes the ENAMETOOLONG issue.

Install via: claude plugin add github:ash1794/vibe-engineering

Fixes #1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: atomic WS pool reservation to prevent concurrent frame interleaving (bd-78b6) (#17)

* fix: atomic WS pool reservation to prevent concurrent frame interleaving (bd-78b6)

- _get_or_create() now sets in_use=True under pool lock before returning
- Wait-on-busy with connect_timeout deadline prevents duplicate readers
- _evict_oldest_unlocked() skips in-use entries (idle-only eviction)
- send_message_streaming() finally block covers full send+recv path
- 17 tests (5 new) covering atomic reserve, busy-wait, timeout, eviction

* fix: enforce max_connections limit and clarify race fallback path (bd-5i2b, bd-mv4o)

- _evict_oldest_unlocked() now returns bool indicating eviction success
- _get_or_create() waits/timeouts instead of exceeding pool cap when all
  entries are in-use and no eviction is possible
- Added explicit comment on race fallback path for readability
- 19 tests (2 new) covering capacity enforcement and eviction return value

commit.message:
🐛 fix(register): use window.location.href instead of router.push — fixes black screen on mobile

router.push() does a client-side navigation: Next.js downloads the RSC
payload as JSON and React renders it on the client. On slow mobile CPUs
this hydration fails silently, leaving only the dark body background visible.

window.location.href forces a hard navigation: the server SSR-renders the
full /onboarding HTML, the browser displays it immediately before React
even hydrates. Guaranteed to work on all devices regardless of CPU speed.

commit.message:
fix: skip Mode.SUBTRACT BuildPart results from shape export

When a BuildPart uses mode=Mode.SUBTRACT (nested boolean subtraction),
the subtraction volume was being exported as a separate visible part.
For example, the interior cavity of a hollowed box showed as a colored
shape overlapping the base.

Now the shape scanner checks the BuildPart mode and skips non-ADD
results (SUBTRACT, INTERSECT) since they are operations on a parent
part, not standalone shapes.

commit.message:
Extract melody with PredominantPitchMelodia in worker (#298) (#306)

Add MelodyExtractor service that runs essentia's MELODIA algorithm on
audio buffers and converts the raw pitch contour into discrete note
events (MelodyNote[]). The pipeline applies EqualLoudness filtering,
then quantizes Hz→MIDI, groups consecutive same-note frames, and
filters notes shorter than 50ms.

Extend the spectrogram worker message protocol with a 'melody' request
type that runs extraction in the worker thread. SpectrogramCache gains
an extractMelodyInWorker() method for main-thread callers. Update
essentia type declarations with arrayToVector, vectorToArray,
EqualLoudness, and PredominantPitchMelodia signatures.

17 unit tests cover Hz→MIDI conversion, note grouping, confidence
averaging, minimum duration filtering, and the full extractMelody
integration path with mocked essentia.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HMnHWzwcMfGLdyb5tiCfgS

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
